q1.question: What do you observe at the conveyor zone?

options.label: Product is piling up / not releasing downstream
The roller doesn't spin at all
The whole section is dead — no LEDs lit anywhere
Roller spins but product isn't moving

q_accum.question: Is the zone intentionally holding product (waiting on a downstream stop), or is this unexpected?

options.label: Expected — downstream is stopped/full on purpose
Unexpected — nothing downstream looks jammed

q_sensor.question: Check the Sensor LED on the ConveyLinx module for that zone. What do you see?

options.label: Blinking red
Blinking green/amber
Solid red
Solid green, but nothing is physically there

q_motor.question: Look at the Motor LED for that zone on the ConveyLinx module. What color/pattern?

options.label: Solid red
Blinking red (slow)
Flashing red, and the motor is stopped
Flashing red, and the motor is running / humming
LED is completely off

result.note: Check the 24VDC power supply feeding this zone group first.
This points to a mechanical problem, not a control fault — check for a slipping belt/O-ring drive band, a worn roller, or a broken sprocket/chain (depending on conveyor type).
This is normal ZPA behavior — the zone is correctly holding product because downstream isn't ready. No fault. Clear the downstream condition and the zone releases automatically.

result.search: sensor blinking red arrival jam missing
sensor solid red error state pin

result.note: Clean the photo-eye lens and reflector and confirm alignment. If this is an RT3/MDR roller with its own control card, also check its Photo-eye Error blink code (1 blink).

result.search: motor solid red not connected stalled
motor flashing red stopped short circuit phase

result.note: An LED that's completely off (not even the base module-status LED) usually means no power at all to that module — check the 24VDC supply and the module's power connector before assuming the motor itself is bad.

options.label: Rockwell PowerFlex 525 (small black keypad)
Eaton SVX9000 / SPX9000
Not sure which one
A fault code like F0xx (e.g. F004, F007, F013...)
Display is dark / no power at all
A fault code like F# (e.g. F1, F7, F13...)
Display is dark / no power at all

result.note: PowerFlex 525 has a small LCD screen with up/down arrow buttons directly on the drive door. Eaton SVX9000/SPX9000 has a larger backlit keypad. Check the drive nameplate if you're still unsure — the model number is printed on it.

Read the exact code off the display (e.g. F004) — then type that code into the main search bar, or open the VFD Faults tab and find it under PowerFlex 525. There are 47 PowerFlex fault codes in the tool (F002-F127).

Read the exact code off the display (e.g. F7) — then type that code into the main search bar, or open the VFD Faults tab and find it under Eaton SVX9000/SPX9000. There are 40 Eaton fault codes in the tool (F1-F56).

q1.question: What's happening at the SLAM / CTM tamp applicator?

options.label: No label picks up onto the tamp pad at all
Label picks up but doesn't transfer onto the carton
Label applies but falls off / doesn't stick
Label applies crooked or off-position
Nothing works — no air movement anywhere

q1.question: What do you see at the IntelliQ ZoneFlex zone?

options.label: Whole zone/section is dead — module GREEN LED is off
Module has power (green on) but the zone won't stop or release product
Detection is erratic — boxes missed or yellow LED flashing
Not sure what the ZFS/ZFA/ZFM LEDs mean

q_detect.question: Watch the module's YELLOW output LED as a carton passes the sensor. Does it toggle cleanly (on when box present, off when clear)?

options.label: Yes — it toggles correctly but the zone still won't index
No / it's erratic or flashing
The green LED is actually off too

q_stopmove.question: Is the zone failing to STOP product (no accumulation) or failing to RELEASE it (product sits, won't index forward)?

options.label: Won't RELEASE — product just sits
Won't STOP — product runs through / no accumulation

result.search: green led off no power dead zone voltage wiring
yellow led flashing erratic missed detection clean align teach
what are zfs zfa zfm reference definition
zone not accumulating releasing try this first

result.note: Sensor is detecting correctly, so the fault is downstream logic or the zone drive — not the sensor. ZoneFlex is zero-pressure: a zone only releases after the zone immediately downstream reports EMPTY. Check the downstream zone is clear and healthy, then check that zone's brake/drive (air-actuated brake or motor-driven roller depending on line type).
